content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to prepare a special dish. There is a special ingredient Chef needs for this dish, so he decided to search for it in all of Chefland. After some research, he discovered it in a secret cave. He may only enter the cave if he has a password; fortunately, a string SSS, which serves as a hint for this password, is written next to the cave.

Chef knows that the secret password is a non-empty prefix of the string SSS. Also, he knows that:


if a prefix occurs in SSS more often as a substring, then the probability that this prefix is the secret password is higher (the probability that a chosen prefix is the secret password is an increasing function of its number of occurrences in SSS)
if two prefixes have the same number of occurrences in SSS, then the longer prefix has a higher probability of being the secret password
Chef wants to guess the secret password with the maximum possible probability of success. Help Chef and find the prefix for which this probability is maximum.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a single string SSS with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one string — the prefix with the highest probability of being the secret password.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Each prefix occurs in SSS the same number of times (once) and the prefix with maximum length is "abc", so the secret password is most likely "abc".
